text: Blessed is the man that walketh not in the counsel of the ungodly, nor standeth in the way of sinners, nor sitteth in the seat of the scornful.
author: Bible

text: But his delight is in the law of the LORD; and in his law doth he meditate day and night.
author: Bible

text: And he shall be like a tree planted by the rivers of water, that bringeth forth his fruit in his season; his leaf also shall not wither; and whatsoever he doeth shall prosper.
author: Bible

text: The ungodly are not so: but are like the chaff which the wind driveth away.
author: Bible

text: Therefore the ungodly shall not stand in the judgment, nor sinners in the congregation of the righteous.
author: Bible

text: For the LORD knoweth the way of the righteous: but the way of the ungodly shall perish.
author: Bible

text: Why do the heathen rage, and the people imagine a vain thing?
author: Bible

text: The kings of the earth set themselves, and the rulers take counsel together, against the LORD, and against his anointed, saying,
author: Bible

text: Let us break their bands asunder, and cast away their cords from us.
author: Bible

text: He that sitteth in the heavens shall laugh: the Lord shall have them in derision.
author: Bible

text: Then shall he speak unto them in his wrath, and vex them in his sore displeasure.
author: Bible

text: Yet have I set my king upon my holy hill of Zion.
author: Bible

text: I will declare the decree: the LORD hath said unto me, Thou art my Son; this day have I begotten thee.
author: Bible

text: Ask of me, and I shall give thee the heathen for thine inheritance, and the uttermost parts of the earth for thy possession.
author: Bible

text: Thou shalt break them with a rod of iron; thou shalt dash them in pieces like a potter's vessel.
author: Bible

text: Be wise now therefore, O ye kings: be instructed, ye judges of the earth.
author: Bible

text: Serve the LORD with fear, and rejoice with trembling.
author: Bible

text: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry, and ye perish from the way, when his wrath is kindled but a little. Blessed are all they that put their trust in him.
author: Bible

text: LORD, how are they increased that trouble me! many are they that rise up against me.
author: Bible

text: Many there be which say of my soul, There is no help for him in God. Selah.
author: Bible

text: But thou, O LORD, art a shield for me; my glory, and the lifter up of mine head.
author: Bible

text: I cried unto the LORD with my voice, and he heard me out of his holy hill. Selah.
author: Bible

text: I laid me down and slept; I awaked; for the LORD sustained me.
author: Bible

text: I will not be afraid of ten thousands of people, that have set themselves against me round about.
author: Bible

text: Arise, O LORD; save me, O my God: for thou hast smitten all mine enemies upon the cheek bone; thou hast broken the teeth of the ungodly.
author: Bible

text: Salvation belongeth unto the LORD: thy blessing is upon thy people. Selah.
author: Bible

text: Hear me when I call, O God of my righteousness: thou hast enlarged me when I was in distress; have mercy upon me, and hear my prayer.
author: Bible

text: O ye sons of men, how long will ye turn my glory into shame? how long will ye love vanity, and seek after leasing? Selah.
author: Bible

text: But know that the LORD hath set apart him that is godly for himself: the LORD will hear when I call unto him.
author: Bible

text: Stand in awe, and sin not: commune with your own heart upon your bed, and be still. Selah.
author: Bible

text: Offer the sacrifices of righteousness, and put your trust in the LORD.
author: Bible

text: There be many that say, Who will shew us any good? LORD, lift thou up the light of thy countenance upon us.
author: Bible

text: Thou hast put gladness in my heart, more than in the time that their corn and their wine increased.
author: Bible

text: I will both lay me down in peace, and sleep: for thou, LORD, only makest me dwell in safety.
author: Bible

text: Give ear to my words, O LORD, consider my meditation.
author: Bible

text: Hearken unto the voice of my cry, my King, and my God: for unto thee will I pray.
author: Bible

text: My voice shalt thou hear in the morning, O LORD; in the morning will I direct my prayer unto thee, and will look up.
author: Bible

text: For thou art not a God that hath pleasure in wickedness: neither shall evil dwell with thee.
author: Bible

text: The foolish shall not stand in thy sight: thou hatest all workers of iniquity.
author: Bible

text: Thou shalt destroy them that speak leasing: the LORD will abhor the bloody and deceitful man.
author: Bible

text: But as for me, I will come into thy house in the multitude of thy mercy: and in thy fear will I worship toward thy holy temple.
author: Bible

text: Lead me, O LORD, in thy righteousness because of mine enemies; make thy way straight before my face.
author: Bible

text: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; their inward part is very wickedness; their throat is an open sepulchre; they flatter with their tongue.
author: Bible

text: Destroy thou them, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions; for they have rebelled against thee.
author: Bible

text: But let all those that put their trust in thee rejoice: let them ever shout for joy, because thou defendest them: let them also that love thy name be joyful in thee.
author: Bible

text: For thou, LORD, wilt bless the righteous; with favour wilt thou compass him as with a shield.
author: Bible

text: O LORD, rebuke me not in thine anger, neither chasten me in thy hot displeasure.
author: Bible

text: Have mercy upon me, O LORD; for I am weak: O LORD, heal me; for my bones are vexed.
author: Bible

text: My soul is also sore vexed: but thou, O LORD, how long?
author: Bible

text: Return, O LORD, deliver my soul: oh save me for thy mercies' sake.
author: Bible

text: For in death there is no remembrance of thee: in the grave who shall give thee thanks?
author: Bible

text: I am weary with my groaning; all the night make I my bed to swim; I water my couch with my tears.
author: Bible

text: Mine eye is consumed because of grief; it waxeth old because of all mine enemies.
author: Bible

text: Depart from me, all ye workers of iniquity; for the LORD hath heard the voice of my weeping.
author: Bible

text: The LORD hath heard my supplication; the LORD will receive my prayer.
author: Bible

text: Let all mine enemies be ashamed and sore vexed: let them return and be ashamed suddenly.
author: Bible

text: O LORD my God, in thee do I put my trust: save me from all them that persecute me, and deliver me:
author: Bible

text: Lest he tear my soul like a lion, rending it in pieces, while there is none to deliver.
author: Bible

text: O LORD my God, if I have done this; if there be iniquity in my hands;
author: Bible

text: If I have rewarded evil unto him that was at peace with me; (yea, I have delivered him that without cause is mine enemy:)
author: Bible

text: Let the enemy persecute my soul, and take it; yea, let him tread down my life upon the earth, and lay mine honour in the dust. Selah.
author: Bible

text: Arise, O LORD, in thine anger, lift up thyself because of the rage of mine enemies: and awake for me to the judgment that thou hast commanded.
author: Bible

text: So shall the congregation of the people compass thee about: for their sakes therefore return thou on high.
author: Bible

text: The LORD shall judge the people: judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity that is in me.
author: Bible

text: Oh let the wickedness of the wicked come to an end; but establish the just: for the righteous God trieth the hearts and reins.
author: Bible

text: My defence is of God, which saveth the upright in heart.
author: Bible

text: God judgeth the righteous, and God is angry with the wicked every day.
author: Bible

text: If he turn not, he will whet his sword; he hath bent his bow, and made it ready.
author: Bible

text: He hath also prepared for him the instruments of death; he ordaineth his arrows against the persecutors.
author: Bible

text: Behold, he travaileth with iniquity, and hath conceived mischief, and brought forth falsehood.
author: Bible

text: He made a pit, and digged it, and is fallen into the ditch which he made.
author: Bible

text: His mischief shall return upon his own head, and his violent dealing shall come down upon his own pate.
author: Bible

text: I will praise the LORD according to his righteousness: and will sing praise to the name of the LORD most high.
author: Bible

text: O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth! who hast set thy glory above the heavens.
author: Bible

text: Out of the mouth of babes and sucklings hast thou ordained strength because of thine enemies, that thou mightest still the enemy and the avenger.
author: Bible

text: When I consider thy heavens, the work of thy fingers, the moon and the stars, which thou hast ordained;
author: Bible

text: What is man, that thou art mindful of him? and the son of man, that thou visitest him?
author: Bible

text: For thou hast made him a little lower than the angels, and hast crowned him with glory and honour.
author: Bible

text: Thou madest him to have dominion over the works of thy hands; thou hast put all things under his feet:
author: Bible

text: All sheep and oxen, yea, and the beasts of the field;
author: Bible

text: The fowl of the air, and the fish of the sea, and whatsoever passeth through the paths of the seas.
author: Bible

text: O LORD our Lord, how excellent is thy name in all the earth!
author: Bible

text: I will praise thee, O LORD, with my whole heart; I will shew forth all thy marvellous works.
author: Bible

text: I will be glad and rejoice in thee: I will sing praise to thy name, O thou most High.
author: Bible

text: When mine enemies are turned back, they shall fall and perish at thy presence.
author: Bible

text: For thou hast maintained my right and my cause; thou satest in the throne judging right.
author: Bible

text: Thou hast rebuked the heathen, thou hast destroyed the wicked, thou hast put out their name for ever and ever.
author: Bible

text: O thou enemy, destructions are come to a perpetual end: and thou hast destroyed cities; their memorial is perished with them.
author: Bible

text: But the LORD shall endure for ever: he hath prepared his throne for judgment.
author: Bible

text: And he shall judge the world in righteousness, he shall minister judgment to the people in uprightness.
author: Bible

text: The LORD also will be a refuge for the oppressed, a refuge in times of trouble.
author: Bible

text: And they that know thy name will put their trust in thee: for thou, LORD, hast not forsaken them that seek thee.
author: Bible

text: Sing praises to the LORD, which dwelleth in Zion: declare among the people his doings.
author: Bible

text: When he maketh inquisition for blood, he remembereth them: he forgetteth not the cry of the humble.
author: Bible

text: Have mercy upon me, O LORD; consider my trouble which I suffer of them that hate me, thou that liftest me up from the gates of death:
author: Bible

text: That I may shew forth all thy praise in the gates of the daughter of Zion: I will rejoice in thy salvation.
author: Bible

text: The heathen are sunk down in the pit that they made: in the net which they hid is their own foot taken.
author: Bible

text: The LORD is known by the judgment which he executeth: the wicked is snared in the work of his own hands. Higgaion. Selah.
author: Bible

text: The wicked shall be turned into hell, and all the nations that forget God.
author: Bible

text: For the needy shall not alway be forgotten: the expectation of the poor shall not perish for ever.
author: Bible

text: By the morning hours
author: Quran

text: And by the night when it is stillest,
author: Quran

text: Thy Lord hath not forsaken thee nor doth He hate thee,
author: Quran

text: And verily the latter portion will be better for thee than the former,
author: Quran

text: And verily thy Lord will give unto thee so that thou wilt be content.
author: Quran

text: Did He not find thee an orphan and protect (thee)?
author: Quran

text: Did He not find thee wandering and direct (thee)?
author: Quran

text: Did He not find thee destitute and enrich (thee)?
author: Quran

text: Therefor the orphan oppress not,
author: Quran

text: Therefor the beggar drive not away,
author: Quran

text: Therefor of the bounty of thy Lord be thy discourse.
author: Quran

text: Have We not caused thy bosom to dilate,
author: Quran

text: And eased thee of the burden
author: Quran

text: Which weighed down thy back;
author: Quran

text: And exalted thy fame?
author: Quran

text: But lo! with hardship goeth ease,
author: Quran

text: Lo! with hardship goeth ease;
author: Quran

text: So when thou art relieved, still toil
author: Quran

text: And strive to please thy Lord.
author: Quran

text: By the fig and the olive,
author: Quran

text: By Mount Sinai,
author: Quran

text: And by this land made safe;
author: Quran

text: Surely We created man of the best stature
author: Quran

text: Then we reduced him to the lowest of the low,
author: Quran

text: Save those who believe and do good works, and theirs is a reward unfailing.
author: Quran

text: So who henceforth will give the lie to thee about the judgment?
author: Quran

text: Is not Allah the most conclusive of all judges?
author: Quran

text: Read: In the name of thy Lord Who createth,
author: Quran

text: Createth man from a clot.
author: Quran

text: Read: And thy Lord is the Most Bounteous,
author: Quran

text: Who teacheth by the pen,
author: Quran

text: Teacheth man that which he knew not.
author: Quran

text: Nay, but verily man is rebellious
author: Quran

text: That he thinketh himself independent!
author: Quran

text: Lo! unto thy Lord is the return.
author: Quran

text: Hast thou seen him who dissuadeth
author: Quran

text: A slave when he prayeth?
author: Quran

text: Hast thou seen if he relieth on the guidance (of Allah)
author: Quran

text: Or enjoineth piety?
author: Quran

text: Hast thou seen if he denieth (Allah's guidance) and is froward?
author: Quran

text: Is he then unaware that Allah seeth?
author: Quran

text: Nay, but if he cease not We will seize him by the forelock -
author: Quran

text: The lying, sinful forelock -
author: Quran

text: Then let him call upon his henchmen!
author: Quran

text: We will call the guards of hell.
author: Quran

text: Nay, Obey not thou him. But prostrate thyself, and draw near (unto Allah).
author: Quran

text: Lo! We revealed it on the Night of Predestination.
author: Quran

text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what the Night of Power is!
author: Quran

text: The Night of Power is better than a thousand months.
author: Quran

text: The angels and the Spirit descend therein, by the permission of their Lord, with all decrees.
author: Quran

text: (The night is) Peace until the rising of the dawn.
author: Quran

text: Those who disbelieve among the People of the Scripture and the idolaters could not have left off (erring) till the clear proof came unto them,
author: Quran

text: A messenger from Allah, reading purified pages
author: Quran

text: Containing correct scriptures.
author: Quran

text: Nor were the People of the Scripture divided until after the clear proof came unto them.
author: Quran

text: And they are ordered naught else than to serve Allah, keeping religion pure for Him, as men by nature upright, and to establish worship and to pay the poor-due. That is true religion.
author: Quran

text: Lo! those who disbelieve, among the People of the Scripture and the idolaters, will abide in fire of hell. They are the worst of created beings.
author: Quran

text: (And) lo! those who believe and do good works are the best of created beings.
author: Quran

text: Their reward is with their Lord: Gardens of Eden underneath which rivers flow, wherein they dwell for ever. Allah hath pleasure in them and they have pleasure in Him. This is (in store) for him who feareth his Lord.
author: Quran

text: When Earth is shaken with her (final) earthquake
author: Quran

text: And Earth yieldeth up her burdens,
author: Quran

text: And man saith: What aileth her?
author: Quran

text: That day she will relate her chronicles,
author: Quran

text: Because thy Lord inspireth her.
author: Quran

text: That day mankind will issue forth in scattered groups to be shown their deeds.
author: Quran

text: And whoso doeth good an atom's weight will see it then,
author: Quran

text: And whoso doeth ill an atom's weight will see it then.
author: Quran

text: By the snorting courses,
author: Quran

text: Striking sparks of fire
author: Quran

text: And scouring to the raid at dawn,
author: Quran

text: Then, therewith, with their trail of dust,
author: Quran

text: Cleaving, as one, the centre (of the foe),
author: Quran

text: Lo! man is an ingrate unto his Lord
author: Quran

text: And lo! he is a witness unto that;
author: Quran

text: And lo! in the love of wealth he is violent.
author: Quran

text: Knoweth he not that, when the contents of the graves are poured forth
author: Quran

text: And the secrets of the breasts are made known,
author: Quran

text: On that day will their Lord be perfectly informed concerning them.
author: Quran

text: The Calamity!
author: Quran

text: What is the Calamity?
author: Quran

text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what the Calamity is!
author: Quran

text: A day wherein mankind will be as thickly-scattered moths
author: Quran

text: And the mountains will become as carded wool.
author: Quran

text: Then, as for him whose scales are heavy (with good works),
author: Quran

text: He will live a pleasant life.
author: Quran

text: But as for him whose scales are light,
author: Quran

text: A bereft and Hungry One will be his mother,
author: Quran

text: Ah, what will convey unto thee what she is! -
author: Quran

text: Raging Fire.
author: Quran

text: Rivalry in worldly increase distracteth you
author: Quran

text: Until ye come to the graves.
author: Quran

text: Nay, but ye will come to know!
author: Quran

text: Nay, would that ye knew (now) with a sure knowledge!
author: Quran

text: For ye will behold hell-fire.
author: Quran

text: Aye, ye will behold it with sure vision.
author: Quran

text: Then, on that day, ye will be asked concerning pleasure.
author: Quran

text: By the declining day,
author: Quran

text: Lo! man is a state of loss,
author: Quran

text: Save those who believe and do good works, and exhort one another to truth and exhort one another to endurance.
author: Quran

text: Woe unto every slandering traducer,
author: Quran

text: In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth.
author: Torah

text: Now the earth was unformed and void, and darkness was upon the face of the deep; and the spirit of God hovered over the face of the waters.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Let there be light.' And there was light.
author: Torah

text: And God saw the light, that it was good; and God divided the light from the darkness.
author: Torah

text: And God called the light Day, and the darkness He called Night. And there was evening and there was morning, one day.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Let there be a firmament in the midst of the waters, and let it divide the waters from the waters.'
author: Torah

text: And God made the firmament, and divided the waters which were under the firmament from the waters which were above the firmament; and it was so.
author: Torah

text: And God called the firmament Heaven. And there was evening and there was morning, a second day.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear.' And it was so.
author: Torah

text: And God called the dry land Earth, and the gathering together of the waters called He Seas; and God saw that it was good.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Let the earth put forth grass, herb yielding seed, and fruit-tree bearing fruit after its kind, wherein is the seed thereof, upon the earth.' And it was so.
author: Torah

text: And the earth brought forth grass, herb yielding seed after its kind, and tree bearing fruit, wherein is the seed thereof, after its kind; and God saw that it was good.
author: Torah

text: And there was evening and there was morning, a third day.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Let there be lights in the firmament of the heaven to divide the day from the night; and let them be for signs, and for seasons, and for days and years;
author: Torah

text: and let them be for lights in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth.' And it was so.
author: Torah

text: And God made the two great lights: the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night; and the stars.
author: Torah

text: And God set them in the firmament of the heaven to give light upon the earth,
author: Torah

text: and to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness; and God saw that it was good.
author: Torah

text: And there was evening and there was morning, a fourth day.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Let the waters swarm with swarms of living creatures, and let fowl fly above the earth in the open firmament of heaven.'
author: Torah

text: And God created the great sea-monsters, and every living creature that creepeth, wherewith the waters swarmed, after its kind, and every winged fowl after its kind; and God saw that it was good.
author: Torah

text: And God blessed them, saying: 'Be fruitful, and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let fowl multiply in the earth.'
author: Torah

text: And there was evening and there was morning, a fifth day.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Let the earth bring forth the living creature after its kind, cattle, and creeping thing, and beast of the earth after its kind.' And it was so.
author: Torah

text: And God made the beast of the earth after its kind, and the cattle after their kind, and every thing that creepeth upon the ground after its kind; and God saw that it was good.
author: Torah

text: And God created man in His own image, in the image of God created He him; male and female created He them.
author: Torah

text: And God said: 'Behold, I have given you every herb yielding seed, which is upon the face of all the earth, and every tree, in which is the fruit of a tree yielding seed-to you it shall be for food;
author: Torah

text: and to every beast of the earth, and to every fowl of the air, and to every thing that creepeth upon the earth, wherein there is a living soul, [I have given] every green herb for food.' And it was so.
author: Torah

text: And God saw every thing that He had made, and, behold, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
author: Torah

text: And the heaven and the earth were finished, and all the host of them.
author: Torah

text: And on the seventh day God finished His work which He had made; and He rested on the seventh day from all His work which He had made.
author: Torah

text: And God blessed the seventh day, and hallowed it; because that in it He rested from all His work which God in creating had made.
author: Torah

text: These are the generations of the heaven and of the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made earth and heaven.
author: Torah

text: No shrub of the field was yet in the earth, and no herb of the field had yet sprung up; for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was not a man to till the ground;
author: Torah

text: but there went up a mist from the earth, and watered the whole face of the ground.
author: Torah

text: Then the LORD God formed man of the dust of the ground, and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life; and man became a living soul.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God planted a garden eastward, in Eden; and there He put the man whom He had formed.
author: Torah

text: And out of the ground made the LORD God to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight, and good for food; the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
author: Torah

text: And a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from thence it was parted, and became four heads.
author: Torah

text: The name of the first is Pishon; that is it which compasseth the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold;
author: Torah

text: and the gold of that land is good; there is bdellium and the onyx stone.
author: Torah

text: And the name of the second river is Gihon; the same is it that compasseth the whole land of Cush.
author: Torah

text: And the name of the third river is Tigris; that is it which goeth toward the east of Asshur. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God took the man, and put him into the garden of Eden to dress it and to keep it.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying: 'Of every tree of the garden thou mayest freely eat;
author: Torah

text: but of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil, thou shalt not eat of it; for in the day that thou eatest thereof thou shalt surely die.'
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God said: 'It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a help meet for him.'
author: Torah

text: And the man gave names to all cattle, and to the fowl of the air, and to every beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found a help meet for him.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; and He took one of his ribs, and closed up the place with flesh instead thereof.
author: Torah

text: And the rib, which the LORD God had taken from the man, made He a woman, and brought her unto the man.
author: Torah

text: And the man said: 'This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.'
author: Torah

text: Therefore shall a man leave his father and his mother, and shall cleave unto his wife, and they shall be one flesh.
author: Torah

text: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.
author: Torah

text: Now the serpent was more subtle than any beast of the field which the LORD God had made. And he said unto the woman: 'Yea, hath God said: Ye shall not eat of any tree of the garden?'
author: Torah

text: And the woman said unto the serpent: 'Of the fruit of the trees of the garden we may eat;
author: Torah

text: but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God hath said: Ye shall not eat of it, neither shall ye touch it, lest ye die.'
author: Torah

text: And the serpent said unto the woman: 'Ye shall not surely die;
author: Torah

text: for God doth know that in the day ye eat thereof, then your eyes shall be opened, and ye shall be as God, knowing good and evil.'
author: Torah

text: And the eyes of them both were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig-leaves together, and made themselves girdles.
author: Torah

text: And they heard the voice of the LORD God walking in the garden toward the cool of the day; and the man and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God amongst the trees of the garden.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God called unto the man, and said unto him: 'Where art thou?'
author: Torah

text: And he said: 'I heard Thy voice in the garden, and I was afraid, because I was naked; and I hid myself.'
author: Torah

text: And He said: 'Who told thee that thou wast naked? Hast thou eaten of the tree, whereof I commanded thee that thou shouldest not eat?'
author: Torah

text: And the man said: 'The woman whom Thou gavest to be with me, she gave me of the tree, and I did eat.'
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God said unto the woman: 'What is this thou hast done?' And the woman said: 'The serpent beguiled me, and I did eat.'
author: Torah

text: And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; they shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise their heel.'
author: Torah

text: Unto the woman He said: 'I will greatly multiply thy pain and thy travail; in pain thou shalt bring forth children; and thy desire shall be to thy husband, and he shall rule over thee.'
author: Torah

text: Thorns also and thistles shall it bring forth to thee; and thou shalt eat the herb of the field.
author: Torah

text: In the sweat of thy face shalt thou eat bread, till thou return unto the ground; for out of it wast thou taken; for dust thou art, and unto dust shalt thou return.'
author: Torah

text: And the man called his wife's name Eve; because she was the mother of all living.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God made for Adam and for his wife garments of skins, and clothed them.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD God said: 'Behold, the man is become as one of us, to know good and evil; and now, lest he put forth his hand, and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live for ever.'
author: Torah

text: Therefore the LORD God sent him forth from the garden of Eden, to till the ground from whence he was taken.
author: Torah

text: So He drove out the man; and He placed at the east of the garden of Eden the cherubim, and the flaming sword which turned every way, to keep the way to the tree of life.
author: Torah

text: And the man knew Eve his wife; and she conceived and bore Cain, and said: 'I have agotten a man with the help of the LORD.'
author: Torah

text: And again she bore his brother Abel. And Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.
author: Torah

text: And in process of time it came to pass, that Cain brought of the fruit of the ground an offering unto the LORD.
author: Torah

text: And Abel, he also brought of the firstlings of his flock and of the fat thereof. And the LORD had respect unto Abel and to his offering;
author: Torah

text: but unto Cain and to his offering He had not respect. And Cain was very wroth, and his countenance fell.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD said unto Cain: 'Why art thou wroth? and why is thy countenance fallen?
author: Torah

text: If thou doest well, shall it not be lifted up? and if thou doest not well, sin coucheth at the door; and unto thee is its desire, but thou mayest rule over it.'
author: Torah

text: And Cain spoke unto Abel his brother. And it came to pass, when they were in the field, that Cain rose up against Abel his brother, and slew him.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD said unto Cain: 'Where is Abel thy brother?' And he said: 'I know not; am I my brother's keeper?'
author: Torah

text: And He said: 'What hast thou done? the voice of thy brother's blood crieth unto Me from the ground.
author: Torah

text: And now cursed art thou from the ground, which hath opened her mouth to receive thy brother's blood from thy hand.
author: Torah

text: When thou tillest the ground, it shall not henceforth yield unto thee her strength; a fugitive and a wanderer shalt thou be in the earth.'
author: Torah

text: And Cain said unto the LORD: 'My punishment is greater than I can bear.
author: Torah

text: And the LORD said unto him: 'Therefore whosoever slayeth Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold.' And the LORD set a sign for Cain, lest any finding him should smite him.
author: Torah

text: And Cain went out from the presence of the LORD, and dwelt in the land of Nod, on the east of Eden.
author: Torah

text: And Cain knew his wife; and she conceived, and bore Enoch; and he builded a city, and called the name of the city after the name of his son Enoch.
author: Torah

text: And unto Enoch was born Irad; and Irad begot Mehujael; and Mehujael begot Methushael; and Methushael begot Lamech.
author: Torah

text: And Lamech took unto him two wives; the name of one was Adah, and the name of the other Zillah.
author: Torah

text: And Adah bore Jabal; he was the father of such as dwell in tents and have cattle.
author: Torah

text: And his brother's name was Jubal; he was the father of all such as handle the harp and pipe.
author: Torah

text: And Zillah, she also bore Tubal-cain, the forger of every cutting instrument of brass and iron; and the sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
author: Torah

text: And Lamech said unto his wives: Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; Ye wives of Lamech, hearken unto my speech; For I have slain a man for wounding me, And a young man for bruising me;
author: Torah

text: If Cain shall be avenged sevenfold, Truly Lamech seventy and sevenfold.
author: Torah

text: And Adam knew his wife again; and she bore a son, and called his name Seth: 'for God hath appointed me another seed instead of Abel; for Cain slew him.'
author: Torah

text: And to Seth, to him also there was born a son; and he called his name Enosh; then began men to call upon the name of the LORD.
author: Torah

text: This is the book of the generations of Adam. In the day that God created man, in the likeness of God made He him;
author: Torah
